diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic diuresis is the hallmark of symptomatic hyperglycaemia.
Frequency overlaps with UTI, but thirst and volume point to osmotic diuresis rather than cystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe vulvovaginal pruritus with thick white discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe/recurrent candidiasis is a recognised marker of glycosuria and hyperglycaemia.
Third-trimester reduced movements is a time-critical warning that mandates CTG assessment; cannot be excluded remotely.
Maternal hyperglycaemia raises fetal-compromise risk, linking the metabolic and fetal concerns.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid excessive maternal weight gain over the past month

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Can reflect fluid retention, polyhydramnios and accelerated fetal growth from hyperglycaemia.

investigation_details.expected_result: markedly elevated, well above target for pregnancy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of hyperglycaemia unquantified; the AI cannot obtain this remotely and must arrange assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation of hyperglycaemia — a clinician/bedside measurement sealed from the AI, which is not reported to have a home glucometer in this encounter.

investigation_details.expected_result: heavy glycosuria, trace ketones, no nitrites/leukocytes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Glycosuria/ketonuria and the UTI screen are unavailable to the AI remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heavy glycosuria plus trace ketones confirms significant hyperglycaemia with early catabolism.
Absence of nitrites/leukocytes argues against UTI as the cause of the frequency.
Large-for-dates uterus supports sustained hyperglycaemia driving fetal overgrowth and excess liquor; a clinician exam finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fetal heart rate reactive on handheld Doppler

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A reactive FHR is reassuring — but it is a clinician Doppler finding the AI cannot obtain, which is exactly why the AI must escalate for a CTG rather than reassure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure not elevated, no proteinuria, no peripheral oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normotension without proteinuria or marked oedema argues against pre-eclampsia; the BP/urine are clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vaginal bleeding, no rupture of membranes, no abdominal pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No watery leak or gush argues against membrane rupture.
No bleeding or severe abdominal pain lowers abruption, though it remains a safety-net concern.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Polyuria/polydipsia + candidiasis + weight gain + glycosuria/ketonuria + high glucose together confirm decompensated gestational hyperglycaemia.
Reduced fetal movements with a large-for-dates uterus in the setting of hyperglycaemia mandates urgent CTG and obstetric review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cardiotocograph (CTG) at a maternity assessment unit
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line objective assessment of fetal wellbeing for reduced movements — the AI cannot perform it, so it must arrange it urgently.
Quantifies hyperglycaemia and guides specialist management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal obstetric ultrasound (fetal growth and liquor volume)
Empirical oral antibiotics for presumed UTI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Frequency here is osmotic diuresis with a negative UTI screen; antibiotics are not indicated and would mislabel the presentation.
